headline: Highway Toll Abolition Costs Government RM3.8B Annually
context: The government's gradual toll abolition programme covering 8 highways by 2026 costs RM3.8 billion per year in compensation to concessionaires, with critics questioning whether the money could be better spent on public transport.

cards.big: RM3.8 billion yearly to abolish 8 highway tolls — but 62% of beneficiaries are T20 car commuters
cards.sub: B40 households with no car derive zero benefit while their tax money subsidises toll-free highways

cards.big: Toll compensation payments exceed combined annual budget for LRT, MRT, and bus service expansion
cards.sub: RM3.8 billion yearly over 15-year concession buyouts totals RM57 billion — more than 3 MRT lines

cards.big: KL-Seremban highway toll abolition increased traffic volume 22% and commute times by 18 minutes
cards.sub: Induced demand means toll removal doesn't improve mobility — it just shifts congestion costs from money to time

cards.big: Concession agreements classified under OSA — parliament cannot verify if compensation rates are fair
cards.sub: PLUS Expressways concession terms remain secret despite RM2.1 billion annual government compensation

cards.big: Paying billions to remove tolls that mostly benefit car owners is regressive policy wearing progressive clothing
Should toll abolition funds be redirected to free public bus networks that serve all income groups equally?
